evaluation.diagnosticOrder: rule order, then affected record identifier
evaluation.duplicatePolicy: one diagnostic per code, affected record, and affected field

rules.description: Board identity, target versions, configuration hash, and eligible lifecycle state are present.
All required normalized configuration checks pass or warn and are bound to the current configuration hash.
Board product and configuration schema versions are present and supported by the target contract.
The migration state is None or Completed and every required migrated-data check passes.
Enabled entity definitions use a supported source type and have current valid metadata evidence.
Required semantic field mappings resolve and source/target data types remain compatible.
Required relationships resolve with supported cardinality, endpoints, and navigation metadata.
Every feature flag required by the selected upgrade path is explicitly enabled.
A current live schema compatibility report exists with zero blockers.
One current valid projection matches the board versions, target projection schema, configuration hash, and source change token.

activationIntegration.validationSnapshotBoundary: Read board aggregate, mappings, feature flags, migration evidence, live schema report, and current projection at one Dataverse consistency boundary.
validationSequence: load target contract and immutable diagnostic catalog
read board aggregate and evidence snapshot
evaluate all rule groups without short-circuiting
persist one validation-result row per diagnostic
update board validation and compatibility status
return validationRunId, configurationHash, targetVersions, allowed, and diagnostics
activationSequence: re-read board lifecycle, row version, configuration hash, validationRunId, and compatibility status
reject a missing, blocked, or stale validation result
rerun compatibility validation when evidence or row version changed
within the activation transaction, change the active pointer only when allowed is true
return a structured denial without mutating lifecycle or active pointers when allowed is false
activationIntegration.staleValidationRule: The validation token is valid only for the same boardVersionId, configurationHash, targetVersions, evidenceFingerprint, and Dataverse row version.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Board activation is blocked by one or more compatibility diagnostics.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Resolve every blocking diagnostic and run validation again.
Generate the missing evidence and revalidate the unchanged board version.

diagnosticCatalog.message: The normalized board configuration is incomplete or invalid for activation.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Correct the reported configuration record or lifecycle state and revalidate.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Validation evidence does not describe the current configuration hash or row version.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Run validation against the current board version before activation.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Product, configuration schema, migration, or compatibility metadata is missing.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Run the compatibility metadata backfill and revalidate.

diagnosticCatalog.message: The board version targets an unsupported CCSB product version.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Migrate through a supported product upgrade path.

diagnosticCatalog.message: The board version uses an unsupported configuration schema version.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Run the supported configuration schema migration and revalidate.
Restore the prior active pointer or deploy an additive forward repair.

diagnosticCatalog.message: A required compatibility migration has not completed successfully.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Complete or repair the migration before activation.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Migrated data failed an integrity or completeness check.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Repair the affected records, rerun the idempotent migration, and revalidate.

diagnosticCatalog.message: An enabled entity definition uses an unsupported source type.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Use a supported Dataverse source type or disable the entity definition.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Mapped entity metadata is invalid or stale.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Refresh source metadata, correct the mapping, and revalidate.

diagnosticCatalog.message: A required semantic field mapping is missing or unresolved.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Create or enable the required mapping and validate its source field.

diagnosticCatalog.message: A mapped source field is incompatible with its target data type.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Correct the source field, target type, or governed transformation.

diagnosticCatalog.message: A required relationship mapping is unresolved or has incompatible metadata.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Correct the relationship endpoints, cardinality, or navigation metadata.

diagnosticCatalog.message: A required compatibility feature flag is missing or disabled.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Enable the flag only after its release gates pass, then revalidate.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Live Dataverse schema validation is missing, stale, or has blockers.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Run the foundation live compatibility gate and resolve all blockers.

diagnosticCatalog.message: The runtime projection is missing, stale, invalid, or bound to an old configuration hash.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Regenerate the projection from the validated configuration.

diagnosticCatalog.message: Projection product or configuration schema metadata does not match the board version.
diagnosticCatalog.recommendedAction: Regenerate the projection from the same validated board version.
